brand.name: Sarah Carroll Design
brand.tagline: Design is as much a matter of finding problems as it is solving them.

offer.what: Freelance graphic design services: logo & brand identity, web design, brochures & collateral, infographics, educational publishing design, business cards, event materials
offer.coreOutcome: A polished, professional visual identity or marketing asset that makes the client look credible and gets noticed — without the client having to figure out design themselves
offer.pricePoint: Unknown — custom quotes, no public pricing; typical freelance project-based fees likely $500–$5,000+ depending on scope
offer.commitment: One-off project basis; no subscription or retainer model detected

audience.icp: Small business owners, nonprofits, and sports clubs within the greater Boston area who need professional design work but don't have an in-house designer
painPoints: Looking unprofessional or inconsistent with DIY design
Too busy to learn design tools or manage a large agency
Unsure how to brief a designer or what to expect from the process
Worried about cost and not knowing if they're getting value
segments: Nonprofits needing event collateral and brochures
Small businesses needing logo/brand identity from scratch
Corporations needing infographics and marketing materials
Sports clubs needing team branding and promotional materials
Educational publishers needing instructional design and infographics

salesCycle.length: Weeks — contact form inquiry → scoping conversation → proposal → project kickoff
salesCycle.trial: None detected; no free consult or discovery call advertised
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Inbound contact form inquiry followed by a proposal/quote conversation
salesCycle.topObjection: Cost uncertainty (no public pricing creates hesitation) and trust/fit (can she handle my specific industry or project type?)

ahaMoment: Receiving the first design concept or logo draft — the moment the client sees their brand come to life visually and feels the creative energy Sarah brings to the collaboration

retention.recurring: None — purely project-based with no subscription, retainer, or membership model
retention.expansion: Return projects (additional collateral, website updates, rebrand phases), referrals to peers in similar organizations
retention.churnRisk: High single-project drop-off; no mechanism to re-engage past clients for follow-on work or keep them warm between projects

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — no sequence to set expectations and build excitement after inquiry
Lead-Magnet Nurture — no lead magnet exists and no nurture sequence for cold contacts
Trial/Consult Conversion — no free consult offer and no email sequence to convert inquiries to paying projects
Booking & Reminders — no automated follow-up or project-start reminders after proposal stage
Value Newsletter — blog is sporadic (last post ~mid-2022); no ongoing educational email for audience
Referral / Advocacy — no structured ask for referrals or testimonials from happy clients
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — no mechanism to re-engage past clients who haven't returned
toneVoice: Warm, collaborative, and creatively passionate. Conversational yet polished — like a thoughtful professional who loves what she does and wants clients to feel genuinely excited about their project. Leans into the emotional and relational side of design, not just deliverables.
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM/GDPR compliance required. No industry-specific compliance constraints (no healthcare, finance, or legal vertical).
